content:
🔥 proxelar | Rust
🎯 Primary Use Case: Intercepting, inspecting, and modifying HTTP/HTTPS traffic for debugging, reverse engineering, testing, and automation.
✨ Key Features: • Lua scripting for request/response modification, blocking, or mocking • Automatic CA generation for HTTPS interception • Forward and reverse proxy modes • Multiple interfaces: Terminal, TUI (ratatui), and Web GUI (axum + WebSocket) • Request filtering and detailed inspection
📖 Summary: Proxelar is a Man-in-the-Middle proxy written in Rust, designed to intercept, inspect, and modify HTTP/HTTPS traffic. It offers flexible interfaces including a TUI and a web GUI, and supports Lua scripting for runtime traffic manipulation.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 uncover | Go
🎯 Primary Use Case: Discovering exposed hosts on the internet by querying multiple search engines through a unified CLI interface.
✨ Key Features: • Query multiple search engines simultaneously • Supports Shodan, Censys, FOFA, Hunter, Quake, ZoomEye, Netlas, CriminalIP, PublicWWW, HunterHow, Google, Onyphe, and Driftnet • Multiple API key input and automatic randomization • stdin/stdout support for input and output • Built with automation in mind for pipeline integration
📖 Summary: Uncover is a Go-based command-line tool that leverages multiple search engine APIs to discover publicly exposed hosts on the internet. It's designed for automation and integration into existing security and reconnaissance workflows.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

💡 reactos | C
🎯 Primary Use Case: To develop a free, open-source operating system that can run Windows applications and drivers.
✨ Key Features: • Windows NT family compatibility (applications and drivers) • Open Source development • Alpha quality, under heavy development • Build environment (RosBE) for Windows and Unix/GNU-Linux • Support for building bootable CD images
📖 Summary: ReactOS is an open-source operating system aiming for compatibility with applications and drivers designed for Microsoft Windows NT family. It is currently in alpha development, meaning it is unstable and best tested in virtual environments.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

💡 mujoco | C++
🎯 Primary Use Case: Accurate and fast simulation of articulated structures interacting with their environment, primarily for research and development in robotics, biomechanics, and machine learning.
✨ Key Features: • C API for low-level control and performance • Interactive visualization with native GUI (OpenGL) • XML compiler for preallocating data structures • Python bindings for ease of use • Unity game engine plug-in
📖 Summary: MuJoCo is a high-performance physics engine designed for simulating articulated structures with contact. It is optimized for speed and accuracy, making it suitable for research in robotics, biomechanics, graphics, and machine learning.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source
